proposed_from.topic: Flagship new topic: support-fragmented causal transport across multiple source trials. Characterize sharp identification of a target-population ATE when no single source trial satisfies positivity on the target covariate support, but the collection of source trials jointly covers the target. The proposed kernel is a finite-support Hall-type if-and-only-if certificate: target ATE is identified exactly when every target support atom can be assigned to a source trial with experimental treatment support and invariant conditional potential-outcome law; below the certificate give a sharp partial-ID interval and explicit two-source witness. This should be positioned against Pearl-Bareinboim multi-transportability and Dahabreh-style multiple-trial transport as a finite positivity/support-fragmentation frontier, not an upgrade of any banked topic.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue signal is the downgraded eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1 proposal, which stated a broad k-source cover idea for general data fusion. This draft differs by fixing randomized source trials, target-population ATE, finite target covariate support, atom-arm source support, an explicit invariance-edge certificate, and a sharp bounded-outcome interval when the certificate fails. doc/API.md has no promoted ExactID or PartialID entry for multi-source trial support fragmentation.","published_axis":"Dahabreh et al. (2020, 2023) supply multi-trial transport identification and estimation under pooled assumptions, and Bareinboim et al. (2013) supply graphical multi-environment transport criteria. The proposed epsilon-gap is the finite-support necessity frontier: exactly which atom-arm source cells are required for point identification, plus endpoint-attaining partial-ID bounds when the frontier fails. Kline and Tamer (2018) and Zivich et al. (2025) motivate partial identification under selective participation or nonpositivity, but do not give the multi-source atom-cover interval."}
seed_list: Characterize exact target ATE identification by an atom-cover certificate across source trials, and prove necessity by uncovered atom-arm countermodels.
Derive the sharp bounded-outcome interval when the atom-cover certificate fails, preserving covered atom-arm means and bounding only uncovered cells.
Construct a two-source binary-atom witness separating single-trial failure, joint-source success, and nonidentification after one invariance edge is removed.
Define an empirical support-certificate diagnostic reporting uncovered target mass and assignment sensitivity for sparse finite-support multi-trial transport.
Promote the atom-cover certificate from ATE-only to joint identification of all target linear contrasts generated by covered atom-arm means.
Give a minimal-source cover number for multi-trial transport and characterize when adding one source strictly shrinks the sharp interval.
Compare Pearl-Bareinboim m-transport formulas with finite potential-outcome atom covers and isolate a non-do-calculus positivity frontier.
Give a closed-form critical uncovered mass at which a target ATE sign conclusion becomes unidentified under bounded outcomes.
Construct a source-assignment sensitivity map showing when multiple admissible covers imply the same estimand but different finite-sample variance targets.
State a finite-support extrapolation impossibility theorem for any estimator that imputes uncovered atom-arm means without structural restrictions.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize exact target ATE identification by an atom-cover certificate across source trials, and prove necessity by uncovered atom-arm countermodels.

anchor_paper.author: Dahabreh, Robertson, Petito, Hernan, Steingrimsson
anchor_paper.claim: Multi-trial causal meta-analysis identifies target population potential outcome means under multi-trial transport assumptions.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: combine information from multiple randomized trials to estimate potential outcome means and average treatment effects

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sufficiency-to-iff strengthened by explicit uncovered atom-arm countermodels, not a routine weakening.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DahabrehRobertsonPetitoHernanSteingrimsson2023 because pooled positivity already identifies the target ATE.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their formula is sufficient; the seed adds atom-arm necessity and below-frontier countermodels on finite support.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity may be left as intuition about uncovered cells.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Necessity is the main conjecture and uses paired laws varying one uncovered target mean.

adversarial_referee.instance: Pearl-Bareinboim graphical completeness may already imply it.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Their object is do-calculus transport over selection diagrams, not finite trial atom-arm positivity.

adversarial_referee.instance: Certificate might be named but not exhibited.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Exhibit 9.1 computes the certificate on a two-source instance.

seed_details.one_line: Derive the sharp bounded-outcome interval when the atom-cover certificate fails, preserving covered atom-arm means and bounding only uncovered cells.

anchor_paper.author: Kline, Tamer
anchor_paper.claim: External-validity randomized-trial data may partially identify ATR and ATE under selective participation.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: RT data need not point identify the ATR or ATE because of selective participation in the RT

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Partial-ID LP/default avoided; interval has closed-form atomwise rectangular representation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Manski-style bounded outcomes because uncovered cells are just missing means.
seed_details.rebuttal: The novelty is the exact covered/uncovered decomposition tied to the multi-source atom certificate.

adversarial_referee.instance: Bounds may be sufficient but not sharp.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Endpoint laws independently set uncovered atom-arm means and prove sharpness.

adversarial_referee.instance: ATE interval may imply other contrasts.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Noted as a separate seed; current draft keeps ATE for the topic-locked qid.

adversarial_referee.instance: Sharp set could be noncomputable.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Equation (1) is a closed-form finite sum.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a two-source binary-atom witness separating single-trial failure, joint-source success, and nonidentification after one invariance edge is removed.

anchor_paper.author: Hotz, Imbens, Mortimer
anchor_paper.claim: External validity across experimental sites depends on overlap and comparability.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: overlap and population differences as central to credible external validity

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample is nondegenerate with positive target masses and randomized source arms.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is only a toy example and cannot carry flagship novelty.
seed_details.rebuttal: It supports the main M7 certificate as a required concrete witness, not as the only flagship claim.

adversarial_referee.instance: Binary support may look hand-tuned.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Both target atoms have positive mass and both observed treatment propensities are nonzero.

adversarial_referee.instance: Witness alone lacks generic-class promotion.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It is paired with Conjecture 1 over arbitrary finite support.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims M8 but gives one witness.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: M8 is supplied by the open finite-support class in Conjecture 1.

seed_details.one_line: Define an empirical support-certificate diagnostic reporting uncovered target mass and assignment sensitivity for sparse finite-support multi-trial transport.

anchor_paper.author: Dahabreh, Stuart
anchor_paper.claim: Reviews methods extending causal inferences from one or more randomized trials and flags overlap limitations.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends population ID to empirical diagnostics; stronger only if uncertainty theorem is added.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an applied diagnostic, not a theorem-level contribution.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; field-tier unless paired with finite-sample validity for estimated support certificates.

seed_details.one_line: Promote the atom-cover certificate from ATE-only to joint identification of all target linear contrasts generated by covered atom-arm means.

anchor_paper.author: Dahabreh, Petito, Robertson, Hernan, Steingrimsson
anchor_paper.claim: Discusses identifiability conditions for target average treatment effects from collections of randomized trials.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: We discuss identifiability conditions for average treatment effects in the target population

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Family unnamed issue becomes the seed; contribution is a joint linear-functional theorem.
seed_details.referee_objection: Once atom-arm means are identified, all linear contrasts are immediate.
seed_details.rebuttal: Likely true; useful as a corollary but not the best flagship kernel.

adversarial_referee.instance: Family is named but may be too automatic.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands partly; keep as secondary, not chosen.

adversarial_referee.instance: No new focal object beyond atom-cover matrix.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Focal object is the covered atom-arm mean vector, but novelty is weaker.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only sufficiency if no necessity for every contrast.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need contrast-specific necessity; not chosen.

seed_details.one_line: Give a minimal-source cover number for multi-trial transport and characterize when adding one source strictly shrinks the sharp interval.

anchor_paper.author: Bareinboim, Lee, Honavar, Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: Multi-environment transportability fuses information scattered across domains to identify effects.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: fusing the observational and experimental information scattered throughout different domains

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Named threshold object, but may be a set-cover restatement unless tied to sharp interval shrinkage.
seed_details.referee_objection: Minimal cover number is combinatorial relabelling of support coverage.
seed_details.rebuttal: Could be nontrivial with interval shrinkage, but weaker than the iff certificate.

adversarial_referee.instance: Cover number may not change causal estimand.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands; downgrade unless interval-shrink theorem is central.

adversarial_referee.instance: Critical threshold not an assumption violation delta.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It is a source-count threshold, so M3 is only approximate.

adversarial_referee.instance: Graphical m-transportability may already solve source selection.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Potentially; not chosen.

anchor_paper.claim: Reduces multiple-domain transportability with limited experiments to symbolic do-calculus derivations.

seed_details.referee_objection: This is an SCM/do-calculus infrastructure comparison outside the CausalSmith theorem substrate.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only viable if framed as an exact-ID positivity frontier consuming graphical transport as comparator.

adversarial_referee.instance: Do-calculus target differs from trial-support positivity.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Risk acknowledged; not chosen as primary.

adversarial_referee.instance: No econometric target beyond comparison.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need the atom-cover theorem as focal object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Completeness paper may close the graphical direction.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Finite potential-outcome support frontier remains distinct.

seed_details.one_line: Give a closed-form critical uncovered mass at which a target ATE sign conclusion becomes unidentified under bounded outcomes.

anchor_paper.claim: Nonpositive transport regions are not nonparametrically identified without additional synthesis or restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity seed has named critical mass and sign flip, but depends on chosen estimand threshold.
seed_details.referee_objection: Critical uncovered mass follows immediately from the partial-ID interval endpoints.
seed_details.rebuttal: Yes, this is a corollary of the sharp interval rather than the main kernel.

adversarial_referee.instance: Critical mass may be descriptive, not necessary.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would define as exact sign-entry threshold from sharp bounds.

adversarial_referee.instance: No new object beyond interval width.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Critical uncovered mass is named, but secondary.

adversarial_referee.instance: Recent positivity paper may already describe this qualitatively.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Quantitative sharp threshold is the gap.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a source-assignment sensitivity map showing when multiple admissible covers imply the same estimand but different finite-sample variance targets.

anchor_paper.author: Su, Rytgaard, Ravn, Eriksson
anchor_paper.claim: Studies efficient estimation of target ATEs using multiple source trials under transportability assumptions.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: semiparametric efficiency and doubly robust estimators for target ATE from multiple source trials

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimator-duality angle is promising but outside the exact-ID locked topic unless variance frontier is formalized.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is estimation after identification, not a support-fragmentation identification theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; field-tier companion unless the assignment map changes the identified estimand class.

seed_details.one_line: State a finite-support extrapolation impossibility theorem for any estimator that imputes uncovered atom-arm means without structural restrictions.

anchor_paper.author: Zivich, Edwards, Shook-Sa, Lofgren, Lessler, Cole
anchor_paper.claim: Transportability positivity violations leave unsupported regions unidentified absent extra assumptions.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: nonpositive regions nonparametrically unidentified without additional synthesis or model assumptions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample promoted to open finite-support class; nondegenerate if target mass and treatment arms are positive.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is Manski missing-cell nonidentification under another name.
seed_details.rebuttal: The chosen proposal embeds it as the necessity side of a multi-trial certificate and sharp interval.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could be a single unsupported atom.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Promote to every failed atom-arm cover with positive target mass.

adversarial_referee.instance: Estimator class too broad to refute without assumptions.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State as identification impossibility, not estimator inconsistency.

adversarial_referee.instance: Negative result alone may lack constructive map.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Pair with sharp interval and atom-cover formula.

proposed_from.literature_map: Cole and Stuart (2010, AJE) standardize one randomized trial to a target population. Westreich et al. (2017, AJE) operationalize transport with inverse odds of sampling weights and sampling positivity. Dahabreh et al. (2020, Epidemiology) identify target ATEs from one trial at a time and pooled collections of trials. Dahabreh et al. (2023, Biometrics) give efficient doubly robust multi-trial estimators under multi-trial transport assumptions. Hotz, Imbens, and Mortimer (2005, JoE) anchor econometric extrapolation across experimental sites through overlap and comparability. Kline and Tamer (2018, Econometrics Journal) give partial and point identification for selective randomized-trial participation. Zivich et al. (2025, JRSS-A) stress that nonpositive transport regions are nonparametrically unidentified without extra assumptions. Degtiar and Rose (2023, Annual Review) survey transport assumptions and diagnostics. Su et al. (2024, arXiv) study semiparametric efficiency for multiple source trials under effect-measure transportability. Bareinboim et al. (2013, NeurIPS) give graphical multi-environment transportability by do-calculus, not finite atom-level positivity. The central tension is sufficient pooled positivity versus a sharp atomwise support frontier. The live gap is a finite-support iff certificate plus sharp below-frontier bounds for support-fragmented multi-trial transport.
